steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase before the blank — ‘the US ______’ — is a noun phrase ending with a noun (‘economist’), and the phrase after the blank — ‘Arthur Laffer’ — is also a noun (a proper name). The blank requires punctuation to connect the job title to the person it identifies.

steps.title: Apply the correct punctuation rule
steps.content: A colon (‘:’) is used to introduce a name or explanation that defines the first noun. Here, ‘Arthur Laffer’ explains who the ‘US economist’ is. Choices B (em dash), D (comma), and A (no punctuation) violate this rule by using incorrect connectors for a definitional pairing. That matches choice C — C is correct.
